crime.value: Murder of Roger Fleming at his home on May 16, 1926, struck and killed with a wheel brush during payment dispute
crime.quote: He went and opened it. . He was found dead the next day by one of his workers.

victim.value: Roger Fleming, potato farmer who habitually cheated vendors by refusing to pay asking price after delivery
victim.quote: Fleming was the type that would place an order and when it arrived refuse to pay the asking price.

closed_world.value: Rural Essex farming community near Chelmsford, England in 1926
closed_world.quote: Roger Fleming a potato farmer in Essex, near Chelmsford, England

culprit_and_motive.value: Virgil Bascomb killed Fleming with a wheel brush during argument over payment for delivered goods
culprit_and_motive.quote: after arguing over the price of a wheel brush, struck and killed Roger Fleming with the wheel brush.

resolution.value: Detective Alistair Basham solves case through evidence analysis, clearing wrongly executed Silas Mercer
resolution.quote: my job was to either provide you with a profile of the criminal or with an identity. I believe I've done

investigator.value: Alistair Basham, criminologist hired by barrister Basil Morgan to clear Silas Mercer's name posthumously
investigator.quote: Morgan, aware of the reputation of Alistair Basham, Criminologist, contacts him about who really killed

suspect_architecture.value: Multiple suspects with differentiated motives: Silas Mercer (wrongly convicted neighbor), Albert Miller (money theft), Henry Bascomb (disabled brush maker), and Virgil Bascomb (actual killer with business dispute motive)
suspect_architecture.quote: Fleming was the type that would place an order and when it arrived refuse to pay the asking price

red_herring.value: Silas Mercer's conviction and execution serves as the primary misdirection - a neighboring pig farmer framed by circumstantial evidence including hog hairs at the crime scene
red_herring.quote: Mercer was tried and convicted and later hanged for the crime

clue_fairness.value: The Berkshire hog hairs are presented with full forensic detail available to reader: three white hairs, three inches long, from snout area, professionally clipped for show purposes
clue_fairness.quote: they were actually hairs from a Berkshire Hog...they had been cut, or clipped if you will

social_world.value: Rural English farming community hierarchy with power dynamics between potato farmers, pig farmers, and traveling craftsmen; legal system's class prejudices leading to wrongful execution
social_world.quote: Roger Fleming a potato farmer in Essex, near Chelmsford, England

alibi.value: Henry Bascomb's physical disability (confined to crutches) serves as his alibi, while his father Virgil's movements during deliveries provide the actual killer's opportunity
alibi.quote: Henry was confined to crutches the rest of his life after getting out of prison

reveal_mechanic.value: Forensic evidence of professionally groomed hog hair contradicts typical farm practices, pointing to brush-making trade rather than pig farming
reveal_mechanic.quote: Farm shows where they show their prize animals

media_and_audience.value: The case exists in public memory through the Mercer children's decades-long fight to clear their father's name, creating ongoing institutional pressure
media_and_audience.quote: Over the years the children of Silas Mercer fought to prove the innocence of their father

investigator_wound.value: Alistair Basham's detachment from emotional investment - he refuses to attend the exoneration court proceedings, treating it as merely professional completion
investigator_wound.quote: my job was to either provide you with a profile of the criminal or with an identity...my job is done

_meta.title: ebook robert f clifton the case of the berkshire hog
